Title: Functional role of FvMdm10 in stress response, pathogenicity, and fumonisins production in Fusarium verticillioides

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: mycotoxin; sterol; neutral lipid storage organelle
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: FB1 is a toxic secondary metabolite; ergosterol is a major fungal membrane sterol; lipid droplets support oxidative stress protection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FvMdm10 positively regulates FB1 production and ergosterol accumulation, maintains normal vacuolar morphology, and negatively affects lipid droplet accumulation; its deletion decreases FB1 and ergosterol but increases lipid droplets and oxidative stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FvMdm10 attenuated vegetative growth, conidial germination, virulence on maize kernels, and FB1 production. 2. ΔFvMdm10 showed decreased ergosterol content but no significant change in ATP content. 3. ΔFvMdm10 accumulated more lipid droplets, displayed abnormal small/irregular vacuoles, and showed increased tolerance to H2O2 and reduced sensitivity to QoI fungicides.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion mutant analysis; complementation; qRT-PCR of fumonisin biosynthetic genes; FB1 ELISA quantification; ergosterol assay; ATP assay; Nile Red staining; CMAC vacuole staining; TEM; pathogenicity assay on maize kernels
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ERMES subunit Mdm10; mitochondrial outer membrane protein
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: mitochondria-endoplasmic reticulum encounter structure (ERMES), mitochondrial outer membrane
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Myro medium for fumonisin induction; H2O2 oxidative stress; NaCl osmotic stress; SDS cell wall stress; azoxystrobin and pyraclostrobin treatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native gene complementation construct; specific promoter not reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; dataset available in ScienceDB reference [53], DOI: 10.57760/sciencedb.21882
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing Fusarium virulence and fumonisin contamination on crops; disruption may lower toxin production but can increase tolerance to oxidative stress and QoI fungicides
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion increases resistance to QoI fungicides and oxidative stress while reducing growth, virulence, ergosterol, and FB1 production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although FvMdm10 affected fumonisin biosynthesis, the paper suggests this occurs indirectly via vacuolar morphology and organelle-function regulation rather than direct enzymatic or transcription-factor action on the FUM pathway.
